reasoning: Treaties requiring complex domestic changes—such as banning research funding, altering patent rights, and prohibiting deployment across multiple domains—take years to negotiate and ratify. According to the search results, there is no international treaty meeting these five stringent criteria that has been drafted, signed, or ratified by any country, let alone by 35+ countries. While there is political momentum (including recent ministerial declarations by AMCEN), these fall significantly short of a signed and ratified binding treaty. With only 24 days remaining before the resolution deadline (Dec 31, 2025), it is practically impossible for a treaty of this magnitude to be drafted, signed, and ratified by 35 countries. The community prediction from late November was 1%, confirming this assessment. I am submitting the minimum allowed probability that reflects this certainty.
